h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: “We will hold the line” is almost two exhibit objects once City Hall or the mill gate gets pinned beside it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to focus on polishing and refining the single-sentence label to resolve its crowdedness.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can see the placard now has two possible readings, not one
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue analyzing the supply-constraint scenario and drafting rules under the tutor's guidance.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: “A shortage explanation may be used only if the stockroom count is low, the delivery cut is independently dated ahead of the price rise, and the margin plus rationing rule show ordinary cover, not a windfall with open buying.”
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully drafts the three gate conditions for the consumer-panel release rule as requested by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The stockroom test has done its job: it can expose theatre. It has not settled the danger that “plausible mechanism” becomes moral cover. So change the job. The shop does not certify its own explanation. Write the consumer-panel release rule that would allow the public label “short shipment explains this rise” only when the conditions can be checked.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges that the previous analytical test does not resolve the learner's moral concern, and shifts the task to drafting a public consumer-panel rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no contingent tutor uptake occurs.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully drafts the three gate conditions for the consumer-panel release rule as requested by the tutor.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no contingent tutor uptake occurs.

The shop does not certify its own explanation. Write the consumer-panel release rule that would allow the public label “short shipment explains this rise” only when the conditions can be checked.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, that is four of the original squares, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to dispute the area calculation and refuse to print.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: pulls the note closer and writes “2 across × 2 down = 4 original squares” above the crossed-out line
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively writes down the correct mathematical representation on the note, demonstrating a shift from skepticism to correct execution.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Leave that one there. Same template, no resizing: does it fit to the right? Now below it.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's skepticism by introducing a physical tiling task with the template, though without an explicit stock-taking statement.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
